teachingHint: Your child is learning the /u/ sound (as in 'up'). It's a short sound, like the start of 'umbrella'. Help them point to each letter, say its sound, then blend: c-u-p → cup, s-u-n → sun. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: c-u-p → Say each sound: c, u, p. Blend: cup
s-u-n → Say each sound: s, u, n. Blend: sun
m-u-m → Say each sound: m, u, m. Blend: mum

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! 'I' says its letter name, not its sound. We say 'eye'. Learn it by sight.

story: I got a cup in the sun. Mum said I can run to get it!

teachingHint: Your child is learning the /u/ sound (as in 'up'). It's a short sound, like the start of 'umbrella'. Help them point to each letter, say its sound, then blend: m-u-g → mug, p-u-p → pup. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: u-p → Say each sound: u, p. Blend: up
m-u-g → Say each sound: m, u, g. Blend: mug
p-u-p → Say each sound: p, u, p. Blend: pup

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! We can't sound this one out. Look at the word and say 'the'. Try to remember what it looks like.

story: The pup got up on the mug. It is up on top!

teachingHint: Your child is learning the /u/ sound (as in 'up'). It's a short sound, like the start of 'umbrella'. Help them point to each letter, say its sound, then blend: r-u-n → run, t-u-g → tug. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: r-u-n → Say each sound: r, u, n. Blend: run
t-u-g → Say each sound: t, u, g. Blend: tug
n-u-t → Say each sound: n, u, t. Blend: nut

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! It looks like it should say 'toh' but we say 'too'. Learn it by sight.

story: I run to tug the nut. It is not an odd nut!

teachingHint: Your child is learning the /u/ sound (as in 'up'). It's a short sound, like the start of 'umbrella'. Help them point to each letter, say its sound, then blend: c-u-t → cut, d-u-g → dug. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: c-u-t → Say each sound: c, u, t. Blend: cut
d-u-g → Say each sound: d, u, g. Blend: dug
m-u-d → Say each sound: m, u, d. Blend: mud

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! The 'o' in 'no' makes a long sound. We say 'noh'. Learn it by sight.

story: I dug in the mud. No, do not cut into the mud!

teachingHint: Your child is learning the /u/ sound (as in 'up'). It's a short sound, like the start of 'umbrella'. Help them point to each letter, say its sound, then blend: s-u-n → sun, c-u-p → cup. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

r-u-n → Say each sound: r, u, n. Blend: run

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! The 'o' in 'go' makes a long sound. We say 'goh'. Learn it by sight.

story: The sun is up! I run to go get a cup.

teachingHint: Your child is learning the /u/ sound (as in 'up'). It's a short sound, like the start of 'umbrella'. Help them point to each letter, say its sound, then blend: m-u-m → mum, t-u-g → tug. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

u-p → Say each sound: u, p. Blend: up
t-u-g → Say each sound: t, u, g. Blend: tug

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! 'Into' is two parts: 'in' and 'to'. The 'to' part says 'too'. Learn it by sight.

story: Mum got up to tug the pup into the den.

teachingHint: Your child is learning the /u/ sound (as in 'up'). It's a short sound, like the start of 'umbrella'. Help them point to each letter, say its sound, then blend: p-u-p → pup, n-u-t → nut. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

n-u-t → Say each sound: n, u, t. Blend: nut
m-u-g → Say each sound: m, u, g. Blend: mug

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! 'I' says its letter name, not its sound. We say 'eye'. Learn it by sight.

story: I got a pup and a nut in a mug. The pup dug it up!

teachingHint: Your child is learning the /r/ sound (as in 'run'). It's the sound at the start of 'robot'. Help them point to each letter, say its sound, then blend: r-a-t → rat, r-i-p → rip. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

r-a-m → Say each sound: r, a, m. Blend: ram
r-a-t → Say each sound: r, a, t. Blend: rat

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! We can't sound this one out. Look at the word and say 'the'. Try to remember what it looks like.

story: The rat ran to the ram. The ram can rip and run!

teachingHint: Your child is learning the /r/ sound (as in 'run'). It's the sound at the start of 'robot'. Help them point to each letter, say its sound, then blend: r-a-g → rag, r-o-t → rot. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

r-o-t → Say each sound: r, o, t. Blend: rot
r-u-g → Say each sound: r, u, g. Blend: rug

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! It looks like it should say 'toh' but we say 'too'. Learn it by sight.

story: I got a rag to mop the rug. Do not let it rot!

story: I ran to the red cup. No, run to the big red mug!
